explanation: The property states that globally, whenever 'control_error is less than 10', it must be the case that 'currentADCSMode is equal to NMF'. This directly translates the 'If A, then B' structure of the natural language into a global implication in LTL.

The natural language statement expresses a global implication: 'If A, then B'. This is directly captured by LTL template 30, which asserts that it is 'Globally true that if bool_exp1 holds, then bool_exp3 must also hold'. Here, 'bool_exp1' is mapped to 'control_error_lessThan_10' and 'bool_exp3' is mapped to 'currentADCSMode_equalTo_NMF'.

The property states that globally (G), whenever the value of 'control_error' is less than 10 (bool_exp2), it must be true that 'currentADCSMode' is equal to NMF (bool_exp3) at that very instant. This accurately captures the 'If A then B' temporal implication.

The property states that at all times (globally), if the condition 'control_error_lessThan_10' is true, then 'currentADCSMode_equalTo_NMF' must also be true. This directly captures the 'If A, then B' causal relationship for all future states.

The statement "If A, then B" translates to the LTL formula G (A -> B), meaning that globally, if A is true, then B must also be true. In this case, 'A' is 'control_error_lessThan_10' and 'B' is 'currentADCSMode_equalTo_NMF'. Template 30, (G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)), perfectly captures this relationship.

The property states that globally (G), if the condition 'control_error is less than 10' (bool_exp1) holds, then the condition 'currentADCSMode is equal to NMF' (bool_exp3) must also hold. This directly translates the natural language statement 'If A, then B' into a global implication 'G (A -> B)'.

The property states that globally (G), if 'control_error_lessThan_10' is true, then 'currentADCSMode_equalTo_NMF' must also be true. This directly captures the conditional 'If A, then B' meaning it must always hold.

The property states that globally, whenever 'control error is less than 10' is true, it implies that 'currentADCSMode is equal to NMF' must also be true at the same moment. This is captured by a global implication (G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)).

The natural language statement describes a global implication: "If P then Q". This is best captured by the LTL pattern G(P -> Q). Template 10, G (bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3), precisely matches this structure. 'control_error_lessThan_10' is assigned to bool_exp2 (the antecedent), and 'currentADCSMode_equalTo_NMF' is assigned to bool_exp3 (the consequent).

The statement "If the value of signal control_error is less than 10, then the value of signal currentADCSMode shall be equal to NMF" describes a universal implication. It states that whenever 'control_error is less than 10' is true, it must always imply that 'currentADCSMode is equal to NMF' is also true. This is best captured by the LTL template G (bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3), where G signifies 'Globally' (always), and '->' signifies 'implies'.
